notes: For BG 6.19, the engineering analog does not contradict the traditional meaning. Reverse-translation of the analog into Sanskrit-domain claims yields a stance compatible with both Shankara and Ramanuja per source pack. The lamp simile (yathā dīpo nivātastho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā) — chapter's most-quoted image. Engineering analog: the senior whose attention does not flicker because the wind has been removed structurally, not through willpower. Shankara/Ramanuja preserved verbatim.
